values: Trekkers not prepared for rapid elevation gain and AMS risk
Trekkers without proper fitness and mental preparation for ascents, descents, and boulder sections

evidence.quote_or_summary: there are ascents, descents, and boulder sections that require proper fitness and mental preparation
Such rapid elevation gain makes you very susceptible to Acute Mountain Sickness.

values: Moderate

evidence.quote_or_summary: Moderate
Although it is rated as a moderate trek

values: Suitable for Fit Beginners

evidence.quote_or_summary: Suitable for Fit Beginners

values: Long trekking days, multiple days almost 10 km long
Last day is a 16 km stretch
Boulder sections begin on the way to Khaari campsite
Continuous boulder fields for over 3 km beyond Khaari
Descent to Patalwansar Lakes from Khari can be tricky
Climb back up the same boulder fields on the way to Dison

evidence.quote_or_summary: There are multiple days that are almost 10 kms long. On the last day, you trek for 16 kms at a stretch.
You start traversing boulder sections on the way to your Khaari campsite. Beyond Khaari, the actual boulder fields begin and you trek through it continuously for over 3 kms.
The descent to Patalwansar Lakes from Khari can be tricky. The next day, on your way to Dison, you need to climb back up the same boulder fields.

values: Initial descent to Dwar from Dison is steep and along a narrow, rocky ledge
Rapid elevation gain of almost 3000 ft over 5 km on day 1

evidence.quote_or_summary: The initial descent to Dwar from Dison is steep and along a narrow, rocky ledge. Although this section is not too long, it can be tricky.
On the first day of your trek, you gain almost 3000 ft in elevation over just 5 km of trekking.

values: Trek Leaders and Technical Guides provide technical gear such as microspikes, gaiters, helmets, ropes wherever required

evidence.quote_or_summary: our Trek Leaders and Technical Guides will provide you with all necessary guidance, support, and technical gear such as microspikes, gaiters, helmets, ropes, etc., wherever required.

values: Highest altitude over 13,000 ft above sea level
Hit 10,000 ft mark on day 1 itself
Rapid elevation gain makes trekkers susceptible to Acute Mountain Sickness (AMS)
AMS can affect first-time and experienced trekkers alike; even fit persons climbing too high too soon are at equal risk
Chances of AMS are real above 10,000 ft

evidence.quote_or_summary: reaching a highest altitude of over 13,000 ft above sea level
You hit the 10,000 ft mark on this day itself. Such rapid elevation gain makes you very susceptible to Acute Mountain Sickness.
At altitudes above 10,000 ft, the chances of being hit by Acute Mountain Sickness are real. Altitude sickness can happen to anybody, first-time trekkers as well as experienced trekkers.
even a fit person climbing too high, too soon runs an equal risk of developing AMS.

values: Sudden rains and snows are a distinct possibility at higher altitudes
Rainfall or snowfall lasting hours can change trek dynamics
Rain can flood campsites or make trails slushy and unsafe
Snowfall can obstruct paths, requiring fresh tracks to be cut
Team may turn back from any campsite if weather deteriorates

evidence.quote_or_summary: at higher altitudes, sudden rains and snows are always a distinct possibility.

Rainfall or snowfall lasting hours can change the entire trek dynamic. Rain can flood campsites or make the trails too slushy to walk safely on, while snowfall can obstruct previously made paths, requiring fresh tracks to be cut on the snow.
If the weather deteriorates, the team may need to turn back from any campsite to ensure the safety of all trekkers.

values: Only exit point is Dawar
Closest hospital is at Dawar with a medical facility for emergencies
Bandipora is the best place for major emergencies
Srinagar is recommended for serious medical attention
Indiahikes team is equipped with high-altitude medical kits, oxygen cylinders, and stretchers
Trekkers must inform Trek Leader of any pre-existing medical conditions

evidence.quote_or_summary: the only exit point is Dawar
the closest hospital is at Dawar. You have a medical facility to handle emergencies.
For any major emergencies, Bandipora is the best place for getting any treatment.
If you require serious medical attention, head to Srinagar for better facilities.
The Indiahikes team is equipped with high-altitude medical kits, oxygen cylinders, and stretchers to handle any altitude-related emergencies.
Please inform your Trek Leader about any pre-existing medical conditions or health concerns, no matter how minor they may seem.

values: Watch out for symptoms of AMS closely due to single exit point at Dawar
Safety is a shared responsibility requiring active participation in safety protocols

evidence.quote_or_summary: the only exit point is Dawar. So you need to be alert and watch out for symptoms of AMS closely.
safety is a shared responsibility. We need your active participation and attention to safety protocols to ensure a safe and successful trek.
